definition: maximum current-noise index in one frequency decade, relative to the applied dc voltage, of a fixed linear resistor

definition_ml.en: maximum current-noise index in one frequency decade, relative to the applied dc voltage, of a fixed linear resistor

remark: The current-noise index 'microvolts per volt in a decade' expressed in dB is the term used to express the 'noisiness' of individual resistors. |The ideal rectangular pass band associated with the current-noise index is one frequency decade, geometrically centred at 1000 Hz (c/s). |The index is defined as follows: |Current-noise index = 20 log_10} (v_rms})/V_T} dB (in a frequency decade). |Where v_rms} is the number of microvolts of open circuit rms current-noise voltage in a frequency decade and V_T} is the number of dc volts applied to the resistor under test. |Since the current-noise power spectrum approximates to a 1/f frequency characteristic, the index provides an estimate of current-noise in any frequency decade.

remark_ml.en: The current-noise index 'microvolts per volt in a decade' expressed in dB is the term used to express the 'noisiness' of individual resistors. |The ideal rectangular pass band associated with the current-noise index is one frequency decade, geometrically centred at 1000 Hz (c/s). |The index is defined as follows: |Current-noise index = 20 log_10} (v_rms})/V_T} dB (in a frequency decade). |Where v_rms} is the number of microvolts of open circuit rms current-noise voltage in a frequency decade and V_T} is the number of dc volts applied to the resistor under test. |Since the current-noise power spectrum approximates to a 1/f frequency characteristic, the index provides an estimate of current-noise in any frequency decade.
